title: Grossularia cognata
taxonomy: Grossularia cognata is classified within the plant family Grossulariaceae. This family is part of the larger order Rosales, which includes various types of flowering plants. Grossularia cognata is a synonym for Ribes cognatum, which is a species of flowering plant. It is native to the southwestern parts of South America. Grossularia cognata is also known as a type of gooseberry due to its fruit-bearing properties.

side_effects: Grossularia cognata, also known as Currant, is not typically associated with adverse side effects when consumed in moderation. However, eating large quantities of the fruit can cause stomach upset, diarrhea, or allergic reactions in some individuals. Grossularia cognata may also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, due to its high vitamin K content. Furthermore, the plant's seeds and leaves are considered toxic and should be avoided. In general, proper identification and preparation of Grossularia cognata are essential to minimize potential risks.
